user_system_path: #364/#366 moved the 15-min heartbeat to a free external cron hitting /api/cron/workday-presence-trigger-runner (GitHub Actions is capped on this private repo). But that external cron requires owner setup and, as of #368, the brain has still fired zero real interventions in production (tkg_actions workday_presence still 4 synthetic rows, last 2026-06-16). Add a zero-config daily floor: chain the existing trigger-runner route into the already-paid, already-running Vercel morning-pipeline cron as a 4th stage so the runner fires at least daily with no additional owner setup, under the external cron rather than replacing it.
forbidden_files_raw: No schema, no UI, no e2e tests, no dependency changes.
required_local_proof: npx vitest run app/api/cron/morning-pipeline lib/workday-presence/__tests__/commitment-bridge.test.ts lib/workday-presence/__tests__/trigger-runner-commitment-bridge.test.ts && npm run build && npm run gate:continuity
acceptance_condition: The trigger-runner runs as a stage of the already-paid Vercel morning-pipeline cron, so a lapsing-commitment intervention is evaluated at least once per day independent of the owner-configured external cron. A 'quiet' runner outcome does not fail the pipeline; only a thrown runner error degrades morning-pipeline to 207.
stop_condition: Stop when issue #368 has a merged PR with gate + build + vitest proof. Production confirmation of a persisted real commitment_lapsing receipt in tkg_actions is BLOCKED_WITH_EXACT_RECEIPT until the next daily Vercel morning-pipeline cron run (0 11 * * *) executes, and requires the owner to have a saved workday_presence_state.
anti_regression_checks: the /api/cron/workday-presence-trigger-runner route and its CRON_SECRET auth are unchanged
next_command: Confirm a persisted real (non-synthetic) commitment_lapsing receipt in tkg_actions after the next daily Vercel cron run; then file the next dead-flag wiring (reply_needed / requires_prep).
